name: Catnip Games SIEM
description: Complete SIEM configuration export for Catnip Games International

streams.description: Stream containing all system events created by Graylog
streams.title: All system events

streams.description: Stream containing all events created by Graylog
streams.title: All events

streams.description: Real SSH authentication logs from WSL machine
streams.title: ssh-auth

streams.description: Contains messages that are not explicitly routed to other streams
streams.title: Default Stream

event_definitions.title: Credential Stuffing Attack
event_definitions.description: Triggers when credential stuffing attempts exceed 20 within 5 minutes from a single IP — indicates automated account takeover attempt
event_definitions.remediation_steps:
1. Block source IP at firewall immediately
2. Force password reset for all targeted player accounts
3. Enable CAPTCHA on login endpoints
4. Check for successful logins following the attack from same IP
5. Review affected usernames for account compromise
6. Add source IP to threat intelligence blocklist
7. Consider implementing account lockout after 5 failed attempts

event_definitions.title: DDoS Attack Detected
event_definitions.remediation_steps:
1. Activate DDoS mitigation rules at network edge immediately
2. Block source IP at firewall
3. Enable rate limiting on affected game server
4. Notify game server team to expect degraded performance
5. Monitor traffic_mbps field to track attack intensity
6. Consider enabling CDN or DDoS protection service (e.g. Cloudflare)
7. Document attack timeline for post-incident review

event_definitions.title: SSH Brute Force Detection
event_definitions.description: Triggers when failed SSH login attempts exceed 10 within 5 minutes — indicates a brute force attack in progress
event_definitions.remediation_steps:
1. Block source IP at firewall immediately
2. Review all affected usernames in the alert
3. Check if any failed attempts were followed by a successful login from the same IP
4. Reset passwords for any successfully compromised accounts
5. Review /var/log/auth.log for full attack timeline
6. Add source IP to threat intelligence blocklist
7. Consider implementing fail2ban to automate IP blocking

event_definitions.title: Suspicious Dev SSH Login
event_definitions.description: Triggers when a suspicious SSH login is detected on any dev server — indicates potential insider threat or compromised developer credentials
event_definitions.remediation_steps:
1. Immediately revoke SSH access for the affected user
2. Check if source IP matches known developer locations
3. Review all commands executed during the session
4. Force password reset for the affected developer account
5. Check for lateral movement to other dev servers from same IP
6. Review git commit history for any malicious code changes
7. Escalate to security team if compromise is confirmed

event_definitions.title: System notification events
event_definitions.description: Reserved event definition for system notification events

elements.title: Dev Environment Security
elements.summary: Developer SSH activity monitoring and suspicious access detection
elements.description: Monitors SSH activity across Catnip dev servers. Shows normal vs suspicious logins, top targeted dev accounts, and suspicious access patterns.

elements.title: Game Server Health
elements.summary: Game server traffic monitoring, DDoS detection, and server health analysis
elements.description: Monitors traffic levels across Catnip game servers. Shows normal vs attack traffic, DDoS events, affected servers, and traffic volume over time.

elements.title: Player Auth Monitoring
elements.summary: Player login success and failure tracking, credential stuffing detection
elements.description: Monitors player authentication events across game servers. Shows login success rates, failed attempts, credential stuffing attacks, and top targeted players

elements.title: Security Overview
elements.summary: High level security summary across all Catnip Games infrastructure
elements.description: Shows total events, event timeline, event type breakdown, and critical event count over 24 hours

elements.title: Sources
elements.summary: This is a list of all sources that sent in messages to Graylog.
elements.description: This is a list of all sources that sent in messages to Graylog. You can narrow the timerange by zooming in on the message histogram, or you can increase the time range by specifying a broader one in the controls at the top. You can also specify filters to limit the results you are seeing. You can also add additional widgets to this dashboard, or adapt the appearance of existing widgets to suit your needs.

elements.title: SSH Monitoring
elements.summary: SSH authentication activity, brute force detection, and attacker analysis
elements.description: Real SSH authentication logs from WSL machine. Shows failed logins over time, top attacking IPs, most targeted usernames, and success vs failure ratio.

notifications.title: Catnip SIEM Email Notification
notifications.description: Sends email notification to Adebowale when any Catnip SIEM alert is triggered

config.subject: Graylog event notification: ${event_definition_title}
config.html_body_template:
Event Definition
Title${event_definition_title}
Description${event_definition_description}
Type${event_definition_type}

Event
Alert Replay${http_external_uri}alerts/${event.id}/replay-search
Timestamp${event.timestamp}
Message${event.message}
Source${event.source}
Key${event.key}
Priority${event.priority}
Alert${event.alert}
Timestamp Processing${event.timestamp}
Timerange Start${event.timerange_start}
Timerange End${event.timerange_end}
Source Streams${event.source_streams}
Fields
    ${foreach event.fields field}
  • ${field.key}:${field.value}
    • ${end}
    ${if backlog}
    ${foreach backlog message} ${end}
    Backlog (Last messages accounting for this alert)
    ${message}
    ${end}
